questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the Ganga is revered as both a sacred river and what else?
questions.explanation: Ganga is venerated as both a holy river and a goddess in Hindu tradition.

questions.q: According to the Puranas, in whose matted locks did the Ganga land to break her fall as she descended to earth?
questions.explanation: Shiva caught the descending Ganga in his matted locks to soften her fall to the earth.

questions.q: Which king's intense penance is said to have won the Ganga's descent to earth?
questions.explanation: King Bhagiratha's penance is credited with bringing the Ganga down from heaven to earth.

questions.q: Bhagiratha sought the Ganga's descent in order to liberate the souls of whom?
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha brought the Ganga to earth so her waters could liberate the ashes of his ancestors, the sons of Sagara.

questions.q: From which deity's toe is the Ganga said to flow before her descent?
questions.explanation: The Puranas describe the celestial Ganga as flowing from the toe of Vishnu.

questions.q: In which major epic is the famous account of the Ganga's descent recounted?
questions.explanation: The Ramayana recounts the descent of the Ganga as part of the story of Bhagiratha.

questions.q: What is the Ganga held to do for those who bathe in her waters?
questions.explanation: The Ganga's waters are held to purify the sins of those who bathe in them.

questions.q: Before descending to earth, through which realm did the Ganga flow according to the Puranas?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is described as flowing through the heavens before her descent to the earthly realm.

questions.q: An epithet often used for the Ganga celebrates her as the celestial river upon what?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is celebrated as the celestial river that came down to flow upon the earth.

questions.q: The waters of the Ganga are especially associated with granting a sanctified passage at what moment of life?
questions.explanation: Ganga's waters are held to grant a sanctified passage at the time of death.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, Bhagiratha sought to liberate the ashes of the sixty thousand sons of which king?
questions.explanation: The sixty thousand sons of King Sagara were burned to ashes, and their liberation prompted Bhagiratha's quest.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which sage reduced King Sagara's sons to ashes when they wrongly accused him of stealing their sacrificial horse?
questions.explanation: When Sagara's sons wrongly accused the sage Kapila of stealing their sacrificial horse, his glance reduced them to ashes.

questions.q: Because Shiva bears the Ganga in his hair, he is honored by which of these names?
questions.explanation: Shiva is called Gangadhara, meaning the bearer of the Ganga.

questions.q: Bhagiratha first performed austerities to obtain the Ganga's release, but a second penance was needed to persuade which deity to receive her fall?
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha had to perform further penance to persuade Shiva to catch the Ganga's overwhelming descent.

questions.q: The Ganga is often depicted in iconography riding upon which animal as her mount?
questions.explanation: The goddess Ganga is traditionally depicted riding a makara, a crocodile-like aquatic creature.

questions.q: Which holy city, sacred to Shiva, sits on the banks of the Ganga and is famed as a place of liberation?
questions.explanation: Kashi (Varanasi), sacred to Shiva, stands on the banks of the Ganga and is famed as a city of liberation.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, the Ganga is described as the mother of which great warrior, born of King Shantanu?
questions.explanation: In the Mahabharata, Ganga is the mother of Bhishma, whom she bore to King Shantanu.

questions.q: Because she descended through Bhagiratha's effort, the Ganga is also known by which name in his honor?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is also called Bhagirathi, named for King Bhagiratha who brought her down.

questions.q: In Puranic tradition, the descent of the Ganga to earth is reckoned as one of the world's great what?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is honored as the foremost of tirthas, the sacred crossing-places of pilgrimage.

questions.q: The Ganga is frequently grouped with which other great river as the most sacred of India's rivers?
questions.explanation: The Ganga and the Yamuna are revered together among the holiest of India's sacred rivers.

questions.q: In her iconography, the Ganga is often shown holding which object that signifies her flowing, life-giving waters?
questions.explanation: Ganga is commonly depicted holding a water-pot, the kalasha, symbolizing her abundant, purifying waters.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, why was Shiva needed to receive the Ganga rather than letting her fall directly upon the earth?
options: She refused to descend at all
The force of her fall would have shattered the earth
The earth would have evaporated her waters
questions.explanation: The Ramayana relates that the tremendous force of Ganga's fall would have split the earth, so Shiva broke it in his locks.

questions.q: In the Mahabharata, the Ganga drowned seven of her sons in her own waters; who was the eighth son she spared, raised to become Bhishma?
options: The incarnation of the Vasu Dyaus
questions.explanation: Bhishma was the eighth son, the partial incarnation of the Vasu Dyaus, spared by Ganga to live among mortals.

questions.q: Which name of the Ganga refers to her three-fold path, flowing through heaven, earth, and the netherworld?
questions.explanation: Ganga is called Tripathaga, she who travels three paths: the heavens, the earth, and the netherworld.

questions.q: After her descent, the Ganga's waters flooded the sacrificial ground of which sage, who in anger drank her up before relenting?
questions.explanation: The sage Jahnu drank the Ganga when she flooded his sacrifice, then released her, giving her the name Jahnavi.

questions.q: Owing to the episode in which a sage absorbed and released her, the Ganga bears which patronymic name?
questions.explanation: Because the sage Jahnu released her, the Ganga is known as Jahnavi, the daughter of Jahnu.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the Ganga is often counted as a daughter of which mountain king, making her a sister of Parvati?
questions.explanation: The Ganga is reckoned a daughter of Himavan, the mountain king, and thus a sister of Parvati.

questions.q: The Bhagavata Purana traces the celestial Ganga's origin to the foot of Vishnu in which of his incarnations, when he measured the cosmos?
options: Vamana (the dwarf, in his Trivikrama form)
questions.explanation: The Bhagavata Purana relates that the Ganga sprang from Vishnu's foot as Vamana strode across the cosmos in his Trivikrama form.

questions.q: The sacred confluence at Prayaga, hallowed by the Ganga, is the meeting of the Ganga, the Yamuna, and which mythical third river?
questions.explanation: At Prayaga the Ganga and Yamuna are said to meet the unseen, mythical Sarasvati in the sacred Triveni Sangam.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, after his second penance, Bhagiratha led the Ganga across the earth to the ashes of Sagara's sons in which region?
options: The netherworld (Patala), where the ashes lay
questions.explanation: Bhagiratha led the Ganga down into the netherworld where the ashes of Sagara's sons lay, granting them release.
